title: Release v1.0 accessibility-first Latin family (static + variable + WOFF2 + specimen) under OFL
context: With the Latin family drawn (fonts-design-102), reviewed (fonts-test-103), and QA gates in place (fonts-qa-002), M1 culminates in a tagged v1.0 release. Per the Elyos quality bar, a font is 'delivered, not merged': it must pass automated QA, have complete OFL/FONTLOG/provenance artifacts, and achieve at least one real adoption event — a Google Fonts submission opened, or deployment in a real document handed to a beneficiary.
objective: Package and tag v1.0 of the accessibility-first Latin family with static OTF/TTF, variable TTF, WOFF2, type specimen, complete OFL.txt/FONTLOG.txt/provenance.json, and at least one adoption event (Google Fonts submission opened or deployment in a delivered document).
acceptanceCriteria: Tagged v1.0 release includes: static OTF, static TTF, variable TTF, WOFF2, type specimen PDF, OFL.txt, FONTLOG.txt, provenance.json.
All CI/QA gates pass: FontBakery Universal + GF profiles (no FAIL), HarfBuzz shaping diff clean, reproducible build verified.
At least one adoption event is documented: a Google Fonts submission PR opened, or the font deployed in a real delivered document (e.g. a downstream Elyos OER artifact) and handed to a beneficiary.
Non-medical framing text is present verbatim in README, specimen, and usage guide.
FONTLOG.txt credits all contributors (with their consent); provenance.json is complete with all glyph sources recorded.
No secrets, tokens, or API keys are present in the release artifacts or CI logs.
output: A pull request and tagged GitHub release (v1.0) containing the complete accessibility-first Latin family: static + variable + WOFF2, type specimen, OFL.txt, FONTLOG.txt, provenance.json, with at least one documented adoption event.
